title: Bridge Essay: Singularity and consciousness: A neuropsychological contribution.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nondual/Unity** (Phase 16) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Nondual/Unity to mechanistic neuroscience through Singularity, and, consciousness:, A, neuropsychological.

**Singularity and consciousness: Aneuropsychological contribution.** — J Neuropsychol. PMID: 33522716. DOI: 10.1111/jnp.12234. In common sense experience based on introspection, consciousness is singular. There is only one 'me' and that is the one that is conscious. This means that 'singularity' is adefining aspect of 'consciousness'. However, the three main theories of consciousness, Integrated Information, Global Workspace and Recurrent Processing theory, are generally not very clear on this issue. These theories have traditionally relied heavily on neuropsychological observations and have interpreted various disorde
